title: stints: PBP → lineup stint derivation (turn stints-contracts green)
description: Implement nba/stints/derive.py. derive_stints(pbp_events, starters_home, starters_away) -> list[Stint] with quarter, start_clock_s, end_clock_s, home_lineup (frozenset[int]), away_lineup, home_pts, away_pts, possessions, raw_event_ids. Algorithm: init from starters; iterate time-ordered PBP; sub closes+opens; quarter boundary closes+opens (carry forward if no fresh sub data, flag mismatches); Dean Oliver possessions. Pass brutus contracts unmodified. Follow-up bead for unanticipated real-data cases.

title: Research: pseudo-r/Public-ESPN-API endpoints + auth + rate limits
description: GULLIVAN. Question: NBA endpoints exposed by pseudo-r/Public-ESPN-API; how agents use it. Searches: github.com/pseudo-r/Public-ESPN-API, ESPN public API NBA PBP undocumented, rate limits/auth/historical coverage, stint reconstruction libs (nbastatR, nba_api Python, pbpstats). Deliverable: bd memory 'espn-api-refs' — ranked sources + one-line takeaways. No synthesis.

title: CONTRACT: PBP→stint derivation correctness — failing tests written first
description: BRUTUS. Pre-write tests/test_stints.py + tests/fixtures/pbp_minigame.json (3 min, 2 subs, known scoring). Asserts on derive_stints(pbp_events, starters_home, starters_away): (1) stint count matches subs+quarter; (2) 5+5 player_ids; (3) boundaries at subs+quarter ends; (4) scoring attributed to active stint; (5) possessions ~= FGA + 0.44*FTA + TO - ORB (Oliver ±1); (6) sum(stint pts_home)==box pts_home; (7) no stint crosses quarter; (8) sub mid-possession: stint ends, possession attributed to stint at possession start. Save CONTRACT_STINTS.md. Confirm red.

title: nba CLI skeleton (Typer) — turn cli-contracts green with stubs
description: Implement nba/cli/main.py with Typer. JSON-by-default; --human flag. Subcommands: schema [--table], sql Q, lineup stats --players --season, sim --team1 --team2 [--no-scouting][--human], players show. Pydantic output models in nba/contracts.py. Typed errors: InvalidPlayerError(3), InsufficientDataError(5), EraOutOfRangeError(4), MultiStatementError(2). Stubs with realistic shapes, meta.stub=true. nba.cli.warnings.sparse(n_effective)->dict. teamspec parser in nba/cli/teamspec.py: 'knicks-2024[swap=kat->randle,divincenzo]' -> {team,season,swaps:[{out,in:[...]}]}. Turn brutus tests green without modifying them.

title: CONTRACT: nba CLI JSON output shape — failing tests written first
description: BRUTUS. Write failing pytest in tests/test_cli_contract.py before cli-lane codes. Pin: nba schema (data.tables[], pgvector_dims; --table filters); nba sql Q (rows, columns, row_count; multi-statement rejected exit 2 MultiStatementError); nba lineup stats --players ... --season Y; nba sim --team1 'knicks-2024[swap=kat->randle,divincenzo]' --team2 'pacers-2024' (score, win_prob.ci, matchups, team_edges, scouting_take); --no-scouting omits scouting_take; nba players show; InvalidPlayerError exit 3; EraOutOfRangeError exit 4; sparse warning {code:sparse_data,n_effective}. typer.testing.CliRunner + pydantic in nba.contracts. Confirm red. Save CONTRACT.md.

title: P0: ESPN API coverage validation (one-pager for 2003/2008/2013/2018/2023)
description: Spec literal prereq before any infra work. Deliverable: docs/p0_espn_coverage.md. Use pseudo-r/Public-ESPN-API (gullivan delivers source refs as bd memory 'espn-api-refs'). Pull one Knicks regular-season game from each of 2003, 2008, 2013, 2018, 2023. For each: box score Y/N+URL, PBP Y/N+URL, PBP has subs?, timestamps in quarter+seconds?, roster Y/N, coach Y/N. Save raw JSON under data/fixtures/espn/<year>/<game-id>.json (gitignore large). Recommend training window in conclusion. No full ingest pipeline yet.

title: Postgres schema design (docs/schema.md + migrations/0001_init.sql)
description: Design Postgres schema. docs/schema.md + migrations/0001_init.sql (Postgres 16 + pgvector). Tables: players, teams, games, coaches, coach_games, rosters, pbp_events (players_on_floor jsonb), lineup_stints (home_lineup int[5], away_lineup int[5], pts, possessions), facts (subject_type enum, subject_id, fact_key, fact_value jsonb), sim_cache (lineup1_hash, lineup2_hash, context_hash, model_versions jsonb, result jsonb), embeddings_player (pgvector). Indices: stints(game_id,quarter,start_clock_seconds); pbp(game_id,quarter,clock_seconds); embeddings_player ivfflat cosine; sim_cache UNIQUE(lineup1_hash,lineup2_hash,context_hash); facts(subject_type,subject_id,fact_key). lineup_hash = SHA256 of sorted (player_id|season), hex.

title: Python project skeleton (pyproject, layout, lint, test scaffold)
description: Create the Python project skeleton. pyproject.toml with deps: typer, pydantic, httpx, sqlalchemy, psycopg[binary], pgvector, polars, pytest, ruff, pyright, respx, python-dotenv. Python 3.12+. Layout: nba/{cli,ingest,stints,schema}/, tests/. Makefile with install/test/lint/typecheck/cli. Entry point nba=nba.cli.main:app. .python-version=3.12. README.md stub. Empty placeholder modules ok. Verify: pip install -e . works, pytest runs, ruff check ., pyright . pass.

title: ESPN PBP fetcher module (one-game-at-a-time, on-disk cache)
description: After P0. nba/ingest/espn.py: fetch_boxscore(game_id), fetch_pbp(game_id), fetch_schedule(date). httpx + ESPN rate limits per gullivan. Cache to data/raw/espn/<endpoint>/<key>.json. Tests via respx. Single-game + cache + tests — no batch driver yet.

title: Research: RAPM, stint-level training, Bayesian shrinkage for lineup analytics
description: GULLIVAN clone. RAPM (Myers, Sill), Bayesian RAPM/shrinkage sparse matchups, per-possession lineup models, player embeddings from box-score, Hungarian assignment in basketball matchup. Deliverable: bd memory 'rapm-prior-art' — ranked sources + one-line takeaways. No synthesis. P1.
